example: The diplomatic rapprochement between the two countries ended decades of hostility.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 rapprocher(가까이 하다). re(다시) + approcher(가까이 가다) → 다시 가까워짐 → 화해

example: His plans for the future remained nebulous and undefined.
mnemonic: nebul(구름, 안개, 라틴어 nebula) + ous = 구름 같은 → 모호한. nebula(성운): nebulous, nebulizer

example: The comedian's mordant wit spared no one, including himself.
mnemonic: mord(물다, 라틴어 mordere) + ant = 물어뜯는 → 신랄한. mord(물다): remorse(후회=되물어뜯는)

example: The fecund soil of the river valley supports abundant agriculture.
mnemonic: fecund(다산의, 라틴어 fecundus). fec(만들다): 많이 만들어내는 → 비옥한. fecundity(다산)

example: The cathedral represents the apotheosis of Gothic architecture.
mnemonic: apo(~에서) + theos(신, 그리스어) + is = 신이 됨 → 신격화. theo(신): theology, atheist, monotheism

example: The venality of the judges undermined public trust in the legal system.
mnemonic: ven(팔다, 라틴어 venum) + ality = 팔 수 있는 상태 → 부패. ven(팔다): venal, vendor, vend

example: The defeated general bore the ignominy of surrender.
mnemonic: ig(아닌) + nomin(이름, 라틴어 nomen) + y = 이름에 먹칠 → 불명예. nom(이름): nominal, nominate, ignominious

example: She faced the crisis with remarkable equanimity.
mnemonic: '이퀴너미티' → 'equa(같은)+nimity(마음)' 마음이 항상 같은 평정심!

example: The reprobate gambler squandered his family's entire fortune.
mnemonic: re(반대) + prob(좋은, 라틴어 probus) + ate = 좋지 않다고 판단된 → 타락한. prob(좋은): approve, probity

example: The charity provides free legal services for indigent defendants.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + dig(가지다, 라틴어 egere=필요하다) + ent = 가진 것이 없는 → 빈곤한. indigenous(토착의)와 어근 구별

example: The dictator issued a minatory warning to all who dared to oppose his regime.
mnemonic: min(돌출하다, 위협하다, 라틴어 minari) + atory = 위협하는. min(위협): menace, imminent, prominent(앞으로 돌출된)

example: His specious argument sounded convincing but was logically flawed.
mnemonic: spec(보다, 라틴어 specere) + ious = 보기에는 좋아 보이지만 → 그럴듯하나 잘못된. spec(보다): species, specimen, spectacle

example: Researchers continue to study the etiology of autoimmune diseases.
mnemonic: etio(원인, 그리스어 aitia) + logy(학문) = 원인에 대한 학문 → 원인론. logy(학문): biology, psychology, pathology

example: The bear becomes torpid during the winter months of hibernation.
mnemonic: torp(마비시키다, 라틴어 torpere) + id = 마비된 → 무기력한. torpedo(어뢰=마비시키는 물고기)와 같은 어원

example: His impetuous decision to quit his job left him in financial trouble.
mnemonic: '임페추어스' → 'impet(돌진)+uous' 돌진하듯 성급한!

example: Urban development continues to encroach on wildlife habitats.
mnemonic: en(안으로) + croach(갈고리, 고대 프랑스어 croc=갈고리) = 갈고리로 끌어당기다 → 침범하다. crochet(뜨개질=갈고리)

example: The charity workers importuned passersby for donations all day long.
mnemonic: im(향해) + portun(적절한, 라틴어 opportunus의 반대) + e = 적절하지 않게 요구하다 → 졸르다. opportune(적절한)의 반대

example: The architect adumbrated the design before creating detailed plans.
mnemonic: ad(~쪽으로) + umbr(그림자, 라틴어 umbra) + ate = 그림자를 드리우다 → 윤곽을 그리다. umbr(그림자): umbrella, penumbra

example: Congress voted to impeach the president for abuse of power.
mnemonic: im(안에) + peach(발, 라틴어 pedica=족쇄) = 족쇄를 채우다 → 탄핵하다. ped(발): impede, impediment

example: The parsimonious landlord refused to spend money on necessary repairs.
mnemonic: parsimoni(절약, 라틴어 parcere=아끼다) + ous = 지나치게 아끼는 → 인색한. parsimony(인색)

example: The novel is didactic in nature, teaching moral lessons through its characters.
mnemonic: 그리스어 didaktikos, didaskein(가르치다)에서 유래. 가르침을 주는 → 교훈적인. autodidact(독학자) = auto(스스로) + didact(가르치다)

example: Years of war had created deep enmity between the two nations.
mnemonic: '엔미티' → 'en+mit(보내다)' enemy(적)에게 보내는 적의!

example: The dissolute king squandered the kingdom's wealth on lavish parties.
mnemonic: dis(떨어져) + solut(풀다, 라틴어 solvere) + e = 도덕이 풀어진 → 방탕한. solv(풀다): dissolve, resolve, solution

example: A sense of ennui pervaded the sleepy coastal town during winter.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 ennui(지루함), 라틴어 inodiare(싫어하다, in+odium=증오). 싫증 → 권태

example: The manager's imperious attitude alienated most of the staff.
mnemonic: '임피리어스' → 'imper(명령)+ious' 명령하듯 거만한!

example: The announcement caused a tumult among the assembled protesters.
mnemonic: 라틴어 tumultus(소란), tumere(부풀다). 감정이 부풀어 터지는 → 소동. tumultuous(격렬한)

example: The professor often digressed from the main topic during lectures.
mnemonic: di(떨어져) + gress(걷다, 라틴어 gradi) = 떨어져 걷다 → 탈선하다. gress(걷다): progress, regress, transgress, congress

example: After the scandal, he became a pariah in the business world.
mnemonic: 인도 타밀어 paraiyar(북 치는 사람=하층민)에서 유래 → 사회에서 버림받은 사람

example: Farmers must cope with the vagaries of weather and market prices.
mnemonic: vag(방황하다, 라틴어 vagari) + ary = 방황하는 것 → 변덕. vag(방황): vague, vagrant, vagabond

example: The senator launched a filibuster to delay the vote on the controversial bill.
mnemonic: 스페인어 filibustero(해적), 네덜란드어 vrijbuiter(자유 약탈자). 의회에서 해적처럼 시간을 약탈 → 의사진행 방해

example: The uxorious husband agreed to every one of his wife's demands.
mnemonic: uxor(아내, 라틴어) + ious = 아내에 관한 → 아내에게 지나치게 순종하는

example: The industrious farmer worked from dawn to dusk every single day.
mnemonic: industr(근면, 라틴어 industria) + ious = 근면한. 같은 어근: industry(산업=근면의 결과)

example: The refugees lived in deplorable conditions in the overcrowded camp.
mnemonic: de(완전히) + plor(울다, 라틴어 plorare) + able = 완전히 울 만한 → 한탄스러운. plor(울다): deplore, implore(간청하다=울며 빌다)

example: The picaresque novel follows a clever rogue through a series of adventures.
mnemonic: 스페인어 picaresco, picaro(악한, 건달). 건달의 모험을 다룬 → 악한 소설의

example: The boxer earned the sobriquet 'The Iron Fist' after his knockout victories.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 sobriquet(별명), 원래 soubriquet(턱 아래를 치는 것). 가볍게 치며 부르는 이름 → 별명

example: The region demanded greater autonomy from the central government.
mnemonic: auto(스스로) + nomy(법칙) = 스스로의 법칙 → 자치권. auto(스스로): automobile, autobiography. nom(법칙): economy

example: The company's dilatory tactics frustrated the regulators.
mnemonic: dilat(늦추다, 라틴어 differre) + ory = 늦추는 → 지연시키는. defer(연기하다)와 같은 어원 계열

example: The stringency of the new regulations frustrated many small business owners.
mnemonic: string(조이다, 라틴어 stringere) + ency = 조이는 상태 → 엄격함. stringent(엄격한)의 명사형

example: International comity requires nations to respect each other's legal systems.
mnemonic: 라틴어 comitas(예의바름), comis(친절한)에서 유래. 국제간 예의 → 상호 존중

example: Many ancient customs have fallen into desuetude in the modern era.
mnemonic: de(떨어져) + suet(익숙한, 라틴어 suescere=익숙해지다) + ude = 익숙함에서 떨어진 → 폐용. suet(익숙한): custom(관습)과 어원 관련

example: The medication provides only palliative care, not a cure for the disease.
mnemonic: palli(덮다, 라틴어 palliare) + ative = 덮어서 가리는 → 완화하는. pall(덮다): palliate, pall(관을 덮는 천)

example: The derelict building was a haven for stray cats and pigeons.
mnemonic: de(완전히) + relict(버려진, 라틴어 relinquere) = 완전히 버려진 → 유기된. relinquish(포기하다)와 같은 어근

example: His critics' mockery goaded him into proving them wrong.
mnemonic: 고대 영어 gad(뾰족한 막대). 소를 몰 때 쓰는 뾰족한 막대 → 자극하다, 몰아세우다

example: The hospital takes a holistic approach to patient care, treating mind and body.
mnemonic: hol(전체, 그리스어 holos) + istic = 전체의 → 전체론적인. hol(전체): whole, catholic(보편적)

example: He became morose after losing his job and barely spoke to anyone.
mnemonic: mor(기질, 라틴어 morosus=까다로운) + ose = 까다롭고 우울한

example: The precocious child could read and write by the age of three.
mnemonic: pre(미리) + coc(익다, 라틴어 coquere) + ious = 미리 익은 → 조숙한. coqu/coc(요리하다): cook, concoct

example: The escalation of the conflict threatened regional stability.
mnemonic: escalat(오르다, 라틴어 scala=사다리) + ion = 올라가는 것 → 확대. scala(사다리): escalate, scale

example: The plangent notes of the cello resonated throughout the cathedral.
mnemonic: plang(치다, 라틴어 plangere) + ent = 가슴을 치는 듯한 소리 → 울려 퍼지는. plang(치다): complain, plaintiff

example: The accountant embezzled thousands of dollars from the company over several years.
mnemonic: em(안에) + bezzle(파괴하다, 앵글로-프랑스어 besiler) = 안에서 파괴하다(=몰래 가져가다) → 횡령하다

example: The editor interpolated several explanatory notes into the ancient text.
mnemonic: inter(사이에) + pol(다듬다, 라틴어 polire) + ate = 사이를 다듬어 넣다 → 삽입하다. pol(다듬다): polish
